IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot right now. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center gig pays **$59.18/hr** (10% over scale at $53.80 base), running 5-12s Mon-Fri plus Saturday work. Ermco's Google Data Center in Mooresville is solid outdoor new construction on 5-10s with Saturday hours. Book depth sits around 191 hands against 43 calls—this one's moving.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is the sleeper. Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge Data Center work hits **$44.80/hr** base but the real money is **$100/day non-taxed per diem** (bumps to $150 Sat/Sun) if you're 100+ miles out—and that GPS radius works in your favor. Six-10s schedule, solid incentive pay. 108 hands on the board for 23 calls means you book quick.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** has the cleanest money: Microsoft Mount Pleasant across four contractors all paying **$62.51/hr**—Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, Kelso Burnett. Wisconsin JW required or reciprocal, long sleeves mandatory. 76 hands chasing 11 calls means Book 1 is tight; start date was late June, so mobilize now.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** still delivers. Inglett & Stubbs has two separate gigs (QTS Fayette and Wellstar Paulding Hospital) at **$40/hr**, plus Beltline Electric at Plant Yates hitting **$42.18/hr** on 5-10s Mon-Fri. 232 hands on 39 calls means you're not starving, but scheduling is tight with the 4-10/1-8 rotations.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** is printing money if you're licensed. Summit Line, Rosendin, Cupertino, and B&D all posting **$55/hr** base with **all OT double time** and NM State JIW License EE98J required. Rosendin dangles a **$15/hr incentive** for hitting 90% of 60-hour weeks. Only 114 hands on 10 calls—this board moves.

**The play:** Data centers are vacuuming hands. Mega-projects (Google, Meta, Microsoft) are anchoring the board; traditional commercial work is secondary.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is running hot with 25 calls and ~143 hands deep—the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood through Parsons Electric is eating 84 Inside JW slots at **$44.80/hr** plus **$100/day non-taxed per diem** and weekend incentives ($150 Saturdays/Sundays). Six-tens with a $100 bonus Mon–Fri keeps the work moving. Hunt Electric's Grand Forks Air Base gig tops out at 10 Inside JW slots, same scale, 4-10s with Friday OT. Board's stuffed but book depth is real.

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** has 34 calls chasing 109 hands at **$61.44/hr**—the best scale in this snapshot. New River Electric Corp is running 37 Journeyman Lineman on distribution work with **$125/night per diem** and a $100 bonus; CDL required. Southern Ohio distro and Chillicothe's 4-man crew (JJM Power, 4-10s, $100/day) are solid backup calls.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** shows 10 calls for ~114 hands at **$55.00/hr** with all double-time OT. Summit Line and Cupertino Electric both require the NM State JIW License (EE98J)—no workaround there. Rosendin's Inside work pays **$40 plus $15 incentive** for heavy hours.

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** has 39 calls but only ~59 hands available—a rare inverted ratio. Top scale is **$70.00/hr**, yet most posted work is non-union work: tree trimming, maintenance, telecom. The board's bloated with old postings; real lineman calls are buried.

**LU-342 Greensboro, NC** is skeleton crew: 2 calls, ~52 hands. Enovum Data Center's 30 Inside JW slots at double-time OT on 6-10s is the only real call worth chasing.

Data-center projects are pulling hands hard; 362 calls across 51 locals means work is scattered, not clustered.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-290 Portland, OR** is moving volume with 24 calls hunting 28 hands across the Pacific Northwest. PermaCold and Dr. HVAC are cycling commercial refrigeration and residential HVAC service techs through Portland Metro and McMinnville. Apollo's got intel work in Tigard — 2 BIM detailers needed on the Ronler Acres project if you run AutoCAD MEP and Revit. This board's bread-and-butter is field service, but the CAD slots pay foreman scale if you've got chops.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** is the heavyweight with 19 calls and 46 hands in queue. Kiewit's Fort St. Vrain Powerhouse is the anchor — fitters on nights, 5/9s (5pm–2am, 9.5 hours), **$65/day per diem** on industrial work. Olson Plumbing has Parker Advent Hospital pipefitting on 4/10s. US Eng. Metalworks is staffing the fab shop nights (5/10s, 4pm–2:15am with shift differential). If you can handle a 5am punch and industrial scale, Denver's moving.

**LU-213 St. John, NB** has 8 calls hunting 31 hands — all Irving Oil Refinery work under Stuart Olson, Cam, and Lorneville. Short-term GPMA and NMA agreements, day shift, journeyman pipefitter roles. Solid Atlantic Canada project with real tenure.

**LU-525 Las Vegas, NV** is lean at 6 calls but 32 deep in book — Hansen Mech owns the Strip (Palazzo, Venetian, Hard Rock plumbing; Four Seasons is S3H). Casino work is steady if you're local-ready.

**LU-192 Cheyenne, WY** has 2 calls but **$100/day per diem** on federal work: PMI's combo welders and plumbers at Ellsworth AFB and Fort Meade VA Hospital (both OSHA 10 mandatory). Remote and tight, but the per diem compensates.

Work's clustering around industrial refinery turnarounds and federal infrastructure — book deep where the big projects are.
